body.html: When the verb of a sentence shows that the Subject is the doer of the action, we say it is in the Active Voice.
body.plain: When the verb of a sentence shows that the Subject is the doer of the action, we say it is in the Active Voice.

body.html: When the verb of a sentence shows that something is done to the subject, we say it is in the Passive Voice.
body.plain: When the verb of a sentence shows that something is done to the subject, we say it is in the Passive Voice.

body.html: When a sentence is changed from the Active form to the Passive, the Object to the Active verb become the Subject to the Passive verb. Note this change in the following examples:
body.plain: When a sentence is changed from the Active form to the Passive, the Object to the Active verb become the Subject to the Passive verb. Note this change in the following examples:

rows.plain: The mason is building the wall.
rows.html: The mason is building the wall.

rows.plain: The wall is being built by the mason.
rows.html: The wall is being built by the mason.

rows.plain: The gate was opened by the peon.
rows.html: The gate was opened by the peon.

body.html: Important Points to Note:
body.plain: Important Points to Note:

body.html: The Active Voice is used when the doer (or the Subject) is to be made prominent.
body.plain: The Active Voice is used when the doer (or the Subject) is to be made prominent.

body.html: The Passive Voice is used when the person or thing acted upon (or the object) is to be made prominent.
body.plain: The Passive Voice is used when the person or thing acted upon (or the object) is to be made prominent.

body.html: In Passive Voice the agent with ‘by’ is usually avoided when the Active form has unknown or vague subject; as somebody, they, people, we, one, etc. The agent with ‘by’ is also avoided when it is understood.
body.plain: In Passive Voice the agent with ‘by’ is usually avoided when the Active form has unknown or vague subject; as somebody, they, people, we, one, etc. The agent with ‘by’ is also avoided when it is understood.

body.html: We may report what he said without quoting his actual words. This is called Indirect (or Reported) Speech.

body.html: Synthesis means joining two or more sentences into one new sentence. There are several ways of joining sentences. We can do it by the use of Conjunctions also. You have read about Conjunction in Part 1 and 2.
body.plain: Synthesis means joining two or more sentences into one new sentence. There are several ways of joining sentences. We can do it by the use of Conjunctions also. You have read about Conjunction in Part 1 and 2.

body.html: Study the following sentences:
body.plain: Study the following sentences:

body.html: Night came on. The room grew dark.
body.plain: Night came on. The room grew dark.

body.html: = Night came on and the room grew dark.
body.plain: = Night came on and the room grew dark.

body.html: He is a fool. He is a rough.
body.plain: He is a fool. He is a rough.

body.html: = He is fool and a rough.
body.plain: = He is fool and a rough.

body.html: (or) = He is a fool as well as a rough.
body.plain: (or) = He is a fool as well as a rough.

body.html: (or) = He is not only a fool but also a rough.
body.plain: (or) = He is not only a fool but also a rough.

body.html: (or) = He is both a fool and a rough.
body.plain: (or) = He is both a fool and a rough.

body.html: It will be noticed that the Conjunction ‘and’ simply adds one statement to another. The conjunction ‘as well as’, ‘not only ……….. but also’, ‘both ………. and' so the same work but they are emphatic.
body.plain: It will be noticed that the Conjunction ‘and’ simply adds one statement to another. The conjunction ‘as well as’, ‘not only ……….. but also’, ‘both ………. and' so the same work but they are emphatic.

body.html: Study the following examples:
body.plain: Study the following examples:

body.html: He is slow. He is sure.
body.plain: He is slow. He is sure.

body.html: = He is slow but he is sure.
body.plain: = He is slow but he is sure.

body.html: I was very angry. I kept quiet.
body.plain: I was very angry. I kept quiet.

body.html: = I was very angry, yet/still I kept quiet.
body.plain: = I was very angry, yet/still I kept quiet.

body.html: He failed. He didn’t lose heart.
body.plain: He failed. He didn’t lose heart.

body.html: = He failed, nevertheless he didn’t lose heart.
body.plain: = He failed, nevertheless he didn’t lose heart.

body.html: He was all right. He was tired.
body.plain: He was all right. He was tired.

body.html: = He was all right, only he was tired.
body.plain: = He was all right, only he was tired.

body.html: It will be noticed that the Conjunction ‘but’, ‘yet’, ‘still’, ‘nevertheless’ express a contrast between one sentence and the other.
body.plain: It will be noticed that the Conjunction ‘but’, ‘yet’, ‘still’, ‘nevertheless’ express a contrast between one sentence and the other.

body.html: The Conjunction ‘yet’, ‘still’, ‘nevertheless’ are more emphatic than ‘but’.
body.plain: The Conjunction ‘yet’, ‘still’, ‘nevertheless’ are more emphatic than ‘but’.

body.html: Study the following examples:
body.plain: Study the following examples:

body.html: Make haste. You will be late.
body.plain: Make haste. You will be late.

body.html: = Make haste or you will be late.
body.plain: = Make haste or you will be late.

body.html: Sit quietly. Leave the hall.
body.plain: Sit quietly. Leave the hall.

body.html: = Sit quietly or leave the hall.
body.plain: = Sit quietly or leave the hall.

body.html: (or) = Either sit quietly or leave the hall.
body.plain: (or) = Either sit quietly or leave the hall.

body.html: Do not be a borrower. Do not be a lender.
body.plain: Do not be a borrower. Do not be a lender.

body.html: = Do not be a borrower or a lender.
body.plain: = Do not be a borrower or a lender.

body.html: (or) = Neither a borrower nor a lender be.
body.plain: (or) = Neither a borrower nor a lender be.

body.html: It will be noticed that the Conjunction ‘or’, ‘either ………… or’, ‘neither …………… nor’ expresses a choice between two alternatives.
body.plain: It will be noticed that the Conjunction ‘or’, ‘either ………… or’, ‘neither …………… nor’ expresses a choice between two alternatives.

body.html: ‘either ….. or’ and ‘neither …….. nor’ are more emphatic than ‘or’
body.plain: ‘either ….. or’ and ‘neither …….. nor’ are more emphatic than ‘or’

body.html: Study the following examples:
body.plain: Study the following examples:

body.html: He was disobedient. He was punished.
body.plain: He was disobedient. He was punished.

body.html: = He was disobedient, therefore he was punished.
body.plain: = He was disobedient, therefore he was punished.

body.html: (or) He was disobedient so he was punished.
body.plain: (or) He was disobedient so he was punished.

body.html: I cannot see. It is very dark.
body.plain: I cannot see. It is very dark.

body.html: = I cannot see for it was very dark.
body.plain: = I cannot see for it was very dark.

body.html: (or) I cannot see because it was very dark.
body.plain: (or) I cannot see because it was very dark.

body.html: (or) I cannot see since it is very dark.
body.plain: (or) I cannot see since it is very dark.

body.html: It will be noticed that Conjunction ‘therefore’, ‘so’, ‘for’, ‘because’, ‘since’, join sentences in which one statement is concluded or reasoned from the other.
body.plain: It will be noticed that Conjunction ‘therefore’, ‘so’, ‘for’, ‘because’, ‘since’, join sentences in which one statement is concluded or reasoned from the other.

body.html: Study the following examples:
body.plain: Study the following examples:

body.html: = As soon as train stopped at the platform, the passengers rushed into it.
body.plain: = As soon as train stopped at the platform, the passengers rushed into it.

body.html: (or) No sooner had the train stopped at the platform than the passengers rushed into it.
body.plain: (or) No sooner had the train stopped at the platform than the passengers rushed into it.

body.html: (or) Scarcely had the train stopped at the platform when the passengers rushed into it.

body.html: (or) Hardly had the train stopped at the platform when the passengers rushed into it.
body.plain: (or) Hardly had the train stopped at the platform when the passengers rushed into it.

body.html: (or) When the train stopped at the platform, the passengers rushed into it.
body.plain: (or) When the train stopped at the platform, the passengers rushed into it.

body.html: We have learnt that a Phrase is a group of words which makes sense but not complete sense. A Phrase does not have a predicate.
body.plain: We have learnt that a Phrase is a group of words which makes sense but not complete sense. A Phrase does not have a predicate.

body.html: Examine the group of words in italics in the following sentence:
body.plain: Examine the group of words in italics in the following sentence:

body.html: He has a chain which is made of gold.
body.plain: He has a chain which is made of gold.

body.html: Here, ‘which is made of gold’ is not a phrase since it has a Subject (which) and a Predicate (is made of gold).
body.plain: Here, ‘which is made of gold’ is not a phrase since it has a Subject (which) and a Predicate (is made of gold).

body.html: Since it has a Subject and a Predicate, it looks like a complete sentence; yet it is not a complete sentence because it forms only part of a sentence.
body.plain: Since it has a Subject and a Predicate, it looks like a complete sentence; yet it is not a complete sentence because it forms only part of a sentence.

body.html: Since a group of words which forms part of a sentence, and contains a Subject and a Predicate, is called a Clause.
body.plain: Since a group of words which forms part of a sentence, and contains a Subject and a Predicate, is called a Clause.

body.html: A sentence that has only one clause (one subject + one predicate) is called a Simple Sentences. Following are the examples

body.plain: A compound Sentence is really two simple sentences which are separated by a conjunction (and, or, but, etc.) and a comma.

body.html: A sentence that has one Independent Clause and one or more Dependent Clauses is called a Complex Sentence.
body.plain: A sentence that has one Independent Clause and one or more Dependent Clauses is called a Complex Sentence.

body.html: The Independent Clause is also known as the Principal Clause and the Dependent Clause is also know as the Subordinate Clause.

body.html: Your friends came to your house one day. Your mother was not at home. You prepared tea for your friend, and served them. Now, apply your imaginations and narrate the incident in one paragraph. Give a suitable title.
body.plain: Your friends came to your house one day. Your mother was not at home. You prepared tea for your friend, and served them. Now, apply your imaginations and narrate the incident in one paragraph. Give a suitable title.

body.html: How I Prepared Tea
body.plain: How I Prepared Tea

body.html: Last evening some of my friends came to my house. My mother was not at home. So, I myself had to make tea for my friends. I seated them in the drawing room. Then I went to the kitchen and lighted a stove. I put some water into the kettle and placed it on the stove. After some time, the water began to boil. Now I put some tea-leaves and sugar into the boiling water. Again, I let it boil for a few seconds. Then I added milk to it. The tea was ready to serve. It gave a very good flavour. I poured it into the tea-pot, and covered the tea-pot with a lid. I arranged the tea-pot, cups and saucers in a tray. With great pride I marched with them to the drawing room. There I poured the tea into cups and served it to my friends. Everybody said that the tea was very tasty. It gave me great pleasure and satisfaction.

body.html: One day your mother asked you to get some postage stamps from the Post-Office. You went there on your bicycle. You forgot to lock your bicycle. When you came outside, you found your bicycle missing. Now, describe the incident in about 100 words.
body.plain: One day your mother asked you to get some postage stamps from the Post-Office. You went there on your bicycle. You forgot to lock your bicycle. When you came outside, you found your bicycle missing. Now, describe the incident in about 100 words.

body.html: The Loss of Bicycle
body.plain: The Loss of Bicycle

body.html: My mother wanted to have some postage stamps and envelopes. She asked me to go to the Post-Office and get them for her. I at once took my bicycle and rode on it to the Post-Office. There was a long queue at the counter. I placed my bicycle against the outer wall of the Post Office and joined the queue. Unluckily I forgot to lock my bicycle. When I came back after getting the stamps and envelopes, there was a big shock for me. My bicycle was missing! I enquired from those around, but nobody knew who had taken it. With a sad heart I walked back home. I told mother about the loss of my bicycle. She was very angry with me. She rebuked me for my carelessness. I could only hang my head and listen!

body.html: The Scene on the Railway Platform
body.plain: The Scene on the Railway Platform

body.html: Last Sunday I went to the railway station to see off my platform presented a colourful scene. There were a large number of passengers on the platform. Some of them were sitting on their luggage. Some were just standing and talking. The hawkers and vendors were selling their wares. Coolies were bringing in the luggage on their heads and shoulders. Some railway porters were moving about with their trolleys loaded with bundles and parcels. The train was a little late. Some passengers became impatient. They went to the edge of the platform repeatedly to see if the train was coming. At last, the train came in. It was crowded. Passengers ran from one compartment to the other to find seats. There was a good deal of jostling. I elbowed my way in and was able to get a good seat for my uncle. After about ten minutes, the engine whistled and the guard waved his green flag. The train left the station, and now there was calm again.

body.html: A house on Fire
body.plain: A house on Fire

body.html: It was a hot summer night. I was fast asleep on the roof of house. my Suddenly I was awakened by loud cries nearby. At once I got up and looked around. I saw thick smoke and flames rising from my neighbour's house. Oh, it was Mr. Chopra's house on fire! I went running into the street. A large number of people had already gathered there. They were trying to put out the fire. Some of them brought water in buckets and threw it on the flames. But the fire was in full rage. I ran back to my house and rang up the fire-brigade. They reached the place in no time. At once they set to their work. For half an hour they kept fighting with the flames. At last, the fire was brought under control. There was a big loss of Mr. Chopra's goods. Luckily there was no loss of life. The cause of fire was a burning cigarette end thrown by Mr. Chopra.

body.html: My First Day at the New School
body.plain: My First Day at the New School

body.html: My father was transferred from Delhi to Aligarh. The whole family migrated with him, and I had to join a new school there. Father took me to the new school. It was my first day there. We went to the headmaster’s office. The headmaster was a kindly looking person. He at once agreed to admit me. He gave us an admission filled it in, and the headmaster put his 'yes' on it. Then we went to the class-teacher. He charged the fees, entered my name in the register and gave me a seat on one of the front benches. After this, father went back home. There were about forty boys and girls in this class. They looked at me with a friendly smile. We studied in the class till the recess period. During interval we went to the school garden. There I made friends with some of my class-fellows. Again, there was a bell and we went back to our class-room. The last bell went at 2 P.M. I came back home. I told my parents how greatly I had liked my new school. They were happy to know all about it.

body.html: Dinner in the Dark
body.plain: Dinner in the Dark

body.html: Last Sunday I invited some of my friends to dinner. They came at about 8 p.m. We sat in the drawing room and chatted for some time. Mother called us after she had laid the table. I took my friends to the dining-room. A pleasant smell of nicely cooked dishes welcomed us. We set to eating as soon as we took our seats. When the eating was in full swing, electricity failed. It became pitch dark in the room. Nothing was visible. We felt the things with our hands and continued eating. It was a bit of fun to eat in the dark. Mother tried to find some candle, but there wasn't any in the house. I wanted to have water and tried to look for a glass on the table. My hand struck against a glass full of water, and it tumbled down. Water started flowing down the table. Everyone got up from his seat to escape getting wet. Just then, the lights came. We shouted with joy and started eating again.

body.html: Mahatma Gandhi
body.plain: Mahatma Gandhi

body.html: Mahatma Gandhi was one of the greatest sons of Mother India. He was born on 2 October, 1869. His real name was Mohandas Karamchand Gandhi. He loved truth and non-violence. He wanted India to be a free country. He was against the British rule in India. So he fought against them, but he did not use any weapons. His only weapon was non-violence. It was more powerful than any other weapon. He forced the British to leave India. He won the hearts of his people. He got them freedom. Gandhiji was a religious person. He had deep faith in God. On 30 January 1948, he was in a temple for his morning prayers. A mad person fired at him. Gandhiji knew that he was going to die, yet he spoke very gently. He had no ill-will against his killer even. He died after a few moments. It was a dark day for India. We can never forget Gandhiji's great services to the nation.

body.html: To Uncle's House on Bicycle
body.plain: To Uncle's House on Bicycle

body.html: My uncle lives in a town that is not far from my village. It is only 8 km. from my village. One Sunday morning, I thought of visiting my uncle. I took my bicycle and started at about 6 in the morning. (The weather was very pleasant. A cool breeze was blowing. Farmers were working in their fields. Birds were making sweet chirps all around. The sun was rising in the east. It was a very charming sight. I rode along the canal that runs by the side of our village. The village children were bathing in it. They splashed water on one another. I enjoyed the beautiful sights of nature all along the way. After cycling for about an hour, I reached the town. There were not many people around. They were still in their homes. The city people are late-risers. When I reached my uncle's house, he had just stepped out of his bath-room. I was in time to breakfast with him. My uncle was much pleased to see me. I passed the day with him, and came back in the evening.

body.plain: PARAGRAPH WRITING

body.html: A paragraph is a number of sentences grouped together and relating to one topic. In order to write a good paragraph on a given topic, the following suggestions should be followed:
body.plain: A paragraph is a number of sentences grouped together and relating to one topic. In order to write a good paragraph on a given topic, the following suggestions should be followed:

body.html: Think deeply on the given topic. Some points will come to your mind. Arrange these points in order so that they can be developed into a paragraph.
body.plain: Think deeply on the given topic. Some points will come to your mind. Arrange these points in order so that they can be developed into a paragraph.

body.html: Now start writing your paragraph. Develop your points one by one into complete sentences.
body.plain: Now start writing your paragraph. Develop your points one by one into complete sentences.

body.html: Take care that each sentence naturally flows from the previous one. Your paragraph should have unity; it should not be just a collection of unrelated sentences.
body.plain: Take care that each sentence naturally flows from the previous one. Your paragraph should have unity; it should not be just a collection of unrelated sentences.

body.html: Your language should be simple but correct. Do not write very long sentences.
body.plain: Your language should be simple but correct. Do not write very long sentences.

body.html: The first sentence of your paragraph should be the topic sentence; i.e. it should at once introduce the main idea of your paragraph. Similarly, the last sentence should state your conclusion on the topic.

body.html: Paragraphs on Personal Topics
body.plain: Paragraphs on Personal Topics

body.html: 1. <u>My Parents</u>
body.plain: 1. My Parents

body.html: I love and respect my parents. They have their own separate qualities. My father is hard and strict. But my mother is very kind and humble. She is deeply religious. She goes to the temple daily. My father rarely goes to the temple. He says that the best temple is man's own heart. My father takes part in politics. He is fond of sports also. But my mother is a domestic lady. She enjoys serving her family. She spends most of her time inside the house. In spite of all these differences in nature, my parents never quarrel. They are very good life-partners. They are ideal parents. They love us and we respect them.

body.html: 2. <u>My Hobby</u>
body.plain: 2. My Hobby

body.html: Reading books is my hobby. I read only good and useful books. I read some good journals also. I get much useful knowledge from them. Reading gives me much pleasure. I can enjoy the company of great writers by reading their works. I can learn from them the good and noble things of life. Good books are the best friends. They contain the wisdom of ages. They are an endless store of knowledge. We can learn much from them. They give us food for the mind. Reading books is the best of all hobbies. It is not very expensive. We can borrow books from libraries.

body.html: 3. <u>My Daily Routine</u>
body.plain: 3. My Daily Routine

body.html: I get up at 5 a.m. daily. I put on my clothes and go out for a morning walk. I return home by 6 o'clock. Then I take my bath. Mother gives me breakfast to eat. After this I take my books and lunch-box. I start for my school at 9 o'clock. I reach the school in about fifteen minutes. The first bell goes at 9-30. I attend all the periods. The school closes at 3 p.m. Then I come back home. I go to the playground in the evening. I play there for two hours. I do my home-work at night. I go to bed at 10 p.m. This is my daily routine.

body.html: 4. <u>My Favourite Games</u>
body.plain: 4. My Favourite Games

body.html: Football is my favourite game. I play this game daily. I play in the school ground. I go there daily in the evening. I play for two hours. It gives me good exercise. I love playing football. This game does not cost much. Two teams can play with just one football. This game is not time-consuming like cricket. A football match takes only one hour. It gives us better exercise than any other game. It is not risky also. There is no danger of any big injury. So, I like this game very much.

body.html: 5. <u>A Pleasant Dream</u>
body.plain: 5. A Pleasant Dream

body.html: Last night I had a very pleasant dream. I saw in my dream that I had won a big lottery prize. My friends congratulated me. My parents were full of joy. They thanked God for my good luck. I asked my father to start some good business. He bought a big shop. He bought a car also. I asked my father to take us on a holiday tour. Just then I heard a knock at the door. I got up and opened the door. My father was standing there. He asked me to get ready for school. That was the end of my pleasant dream.

body.html: 6. <u>How I Spent My Last Sunday</u>
body.plain: 6. How I Spent My Last Sunday

body.html: Sunday is a holiday. It is a day of leisure. There is no hurry or worry. Usually, I get up at 5 a.m. But last Sunday I got up at 7 o'clock. I took a cup of tea and sat down to read the newspaper. Then I went out to play with my friends. I came back at 10 o'clock. I took my bath. Mother gave me breakfast. Then I sat down to do my homework. My friend, Rakesh, came to my house. We took our lunch together. In the evening we went to see a film. We came back at about 9 p.m. I had dinner and went to my bed-room. It was a well-spent Sunday.

body.html: 7. <u>How I Prepared Tea</u>
body.plain: 7. How I Prepared Tea

body.html: One day some of my friends came to my house. My mother was not then at home. I myself had to prepare tea for my friends. I seated them in the drawing room. Then I went to the kitchen and lighted a stove. I measured four cups of water, and put it into a kettle. I put the kettle on the stove. After some time the water began to boil. Now I added to it four teaspoonfuls of sugar. I wanted to make strong tea. So I put into the boiling water a good quantity of tea leaves. I let the water brew for some time. Lastly I added milk to it. Now tea was ready. I poured it into cups, and served it to my friends. Everybody said that the tea was very tasty. I felt proud.

body.html: Paragraphs on Persons
body.plain: Paragraphs on Persons

body.html: 1. <u>The Work of a Postman</u>
body.plain: 1. The Work of a Postman

body.html: The postman is a very useful public servant. His duty is very hard. He has to do his duty in sun and rain. He goes to the Head Post-office in the morning. There he gets the dak. He arranges the letters. He puts them in a bag. Then he goes on his beat. He goes from door to door. He is eagerly waited for. He brings good as well as bad news. He helps to bring the world closer. A postman has to work hard. But his pay is small. He can hardly make both ends meet. He is poorly dressed. I pity his lot.

body.html: 2. <u>The Work of a Constable</u>
body.plain: 2. The Work of a Constable

body.html: The constable is a very useful public servant. He performs many important duties. He keeps an eye on bad characters. He arrests the criminals. He helps to keep peace and order. He controls the traffic. The duty of a constable is very hard. He has to be on the alert all the time. Sometimes even his life is in danger. He has to do his duty at the risk of his life. The constable is an important part of the government. Without his help it is impossible to enforce discipline in society. We should pay due regard to him.

body.html: 3. <u>The Life of a Rickshaw Puller</u>
body.plain: 3. The Life of a Rickshaw Puller

body.html: The life of a rickshaw puller is very hard. It is, in fact, miserable. He lives by the sweat of his brow. He has to work like a beast of burden. He pulls heavy loads. He pulls men, women and children. He perspires from head to foot. It is very painful to look at him. A rickshaw-puller hardly gets as much as he deserves. People try to give him as little as possible. He has to work in sun and rain. He has to work from morning till evening. Even then he gets very little to eat. He is in rags. It is unlucky that even in this age of science men have to work like beasts of burden to earn their bread.

body.html: 4. <u>The Life of an Indian Farmer</u>
body.plain: 4. The Life of an Indian Farmer

body.html: The life of an Indian farmer is very hard. He has to work hard in his fields. He has to work in sun and rain. He has to plough his fields. He has to water them. Then he sows seeds in his fields. He looks after his crops day and night. Harvesting is also no easy job. It is very difficult and tiring. Even after so much labour, the Indian farmer remains poor. He has to live from hand to mouth. His lot is pitiable. But though he is poor, he lives happily. He is simple in his habits. He is pure in his thoughts. He lives away from the evils of city life.

body.html: 5. <u>A Street Hawker</u>
body.plain: 5. A Street Hawker

body.html: Street hawkers are a common sight. They sell little things of daily use. They go from street to street. They shout to attract customers. They are popular with ladies and children. Some hawkers are very talkative. They are very clever. They trap their customers by oily words. They sell inferior goods. They charge very high prices. But some hawkers are very honest. They believe in fair dealings. They do a great service to us. They bring things to our door. We have not to go to the market.

body.html: Paragraphs on Sports & Festivals
body.plain: Paragraphs on Sports & Festivals

body.html: 1. <u>Importance of Games</u>
body.plain: 1. Importance of Games

body.html: Games are a very necessary part of life. They keep us healthy. They refresh our weary minds. They are useful for both our mind and body. Physically we become healthy. Mentally we become disciplined. Games give us pluck and courage. They make us bold and confident. They develop in us a spirit of cooperation. They teach us to fight in a healthy spirit. A player fights with his rival in the playground. But he has no ill-will against him. In short, games help to make, good citizens. Our education is incomplete without them.

body.html: 2. <u>A Football Match</u>
body.plain: 2. A Football Match

body.html: A football match was played between our school and the Government school. It was played on our school ground. I, too, played in this match. First of all, there was a toss. We won the toss and chose our side. The ball was placed at the centre. The referee blew the whistle and the match started. At first the game was slow, but soon it became brisk. Our side played very well. I took the ball near the goal of the opposite side. I passed it to my captain. He gave a good kick. We scored a goal. In the second half neither team could score any goal. We won the match by one goal.

body.html: 3. <u>The Annual Sports Day</u>
body.plain: 3. The Annual Sports Day

body.html: The annual sports of our school were held on the seventh of this month. The Education Minister presided over the function. We reached the ground at 10 a.m. Many students took part in the sports. There were races, jumps and many other interesting events. I took part in the cycle race and the high jump. I stood first in the cycle race. In high jump also I won the second position. The sports were finished by 4-30 p.m. Then there was the prize-giving function. Parents and other important people of the town had been invited. Everybody clapped and cheered when the prize-winners got their prizes. In the end, the Principal thanked the Minister and the other guests. It was a very successful function.

body.html: 4. <u>The Independence Day</u>
body.plain: 4. The Independence Day

body.html: India became a free country on 15 August, 1947. So, August 15 is called the Independence Day of India. The British rule came to an end on this day. It is a red letter day in the history of the country. It is celebrated all over the country with great enthusiasm. On this day all schools, colleges and offices remain closed. It is a national holiday. Public meetings are held in all towns and cities. A big function is held at Delhi. The Prime Minister unfurls the national flag at the Red Fort. Homage is paid to those who laid down their lives for the freedom of the country. Our freedom is a hard-won freedom. We should protect it.

body.html: 5. <u>The Republic Day</u>
body.plain: 5. The Republic Day

body.html: India became a Republic on 26 January, 1950. The Constitution of the country came into force on this day. India became a secular democratic country. The power of government passed into the hands of the common people. All castes, creeds and religions were to be equal in the eye of the law. It is a red letter day in the history of the country. It is celebrated all over the country with great enthusiasm. The national flag is unfurled at all the public buildings. A big function is held in Delhi. The President of the country presides over this function. It is worth-seeing. This day is a national holiday.

body.html: 1. <u>A Hot Summer Day</u>
body.plain: 1. A Hot Summer Day

body.html: June 20 was a very hot day. The sun was in all its fury. It was burning. The earth went dry. There was dust everywhere. The heat was unbearable. There was no stir in the air. Men and animals were panting. All were perspiring. Little children were crying restlessly. Everybody looked for shade. Not a soul was moving out. Bazars and streets looked deserted. All work came to a standstill. We prayed to God for a shower of rain. In the evening the heat lessened a little. We felt some relief. Thank God that it became pleasant at night.

body.html: 2. A Rainy Day
body.plain: 2. A Rainy Day

body.html: It was the month of July. The day was very hot. Men and animals were panting. All were perspiring. We longed for a shower of rain. In the afternoon, some clouds appeared in the east. Soon the whole sky was overcast with dark clouds. It started raining heavily. Streets and bazars were flooded with water. Little children came out and played in the rain. They splashed water over one another. The rain stopped after about two hours. It became very cool and pleasant. Streets and bazars had been washed clean. The city gave a fresh look.

body.html: 3. <u>A Morning Walk</u>
body.plain: 3. A Morning Walk

body.html: Morning walk is the best form of exercise. It costs nothing. It is very useful for our health. It refreshes our mind and strengthens our body. It prolongs our life. It saves us from many diseases. It is equally good for the young and the old. Morning walk keeps us fresh for the whole day. It develops in us the habit of early rising. It awakens in us love for nature. The dew drops, the fresh flowers, the chirping birds and the rustling leaves charm our heart. We start loving the various objects of natural beauty. Thus morning walk is useful not only for our body but our soul also.

body.html: 4. <u>My School Garden</u>
body.plain: 4. My School Garden

body.html: There is a garden in our school. It is a very big garden. It is at the back of the Principal's office. It is a beautiful garden. A gardener looks after it. There are many kinds of flowers in it. There are some fruit trees also. This garden adds to the beauty of the school. The students go there in their recess period. There is a grassy plot in this garden. Students have their lunch there. But they are not allowed to pluck flowers or fruit. Sometimes in winter, classes are held there in the open. I love sitting in this garden. I always go there in the recess period.

body.html: 5. <u>The Platform Scene</u>
body.plain: 5. The Platform Scene

body.html: from one Last evening I went to the railway station to see my uncle off. The train arrived. All the compartments were over-crowded. Passengers ran compartment to another. People who were inside tried to get out. Those who were outside tried to get in. There was a great deal of jostling. Some smart ones elbowed their way in. Everybody was in a hurry. The scene was very colourful. The vendors cried their wares. Their cries were amusing. The waterman served water to thirsty passengers and magazines. A newsboy hurried about selling his newspapers and magazines. After about fifteen minutes, the engine gave a whistle. The guard waved a green flag, and the train steamed off.

body.html: 6. <u>A Visit to a Zoo</u>
body.plain: 6. A Visit to a Zoo

body.html: There is a zoo in our city. I visited it last Sunday. I went with my parents. We bought tickets and went in. First of all we saw birds. There were many beautiful and rare kinds of birds. We saw parrots, canaries, swallows, peacocks, ducks, cranes, herons, gulls and geese. Then we saw some wild beasts. A lioness and her cub were basking in the sunshine. They roared now and then. We also saw wolves, tigers, elephants and rhinos. When we were coming back, we saw a muddy pond. There were many big snakes in it. It was fearful to look at them. We stayed in the zoo for about three hours. Then we came back home.

body.html: 7. <u>A Visit to a Circus</u>
body.plain: 7. A Visit to a Circus

body.html: A circus came to our town last month. I went to see it with my parents. We bought tickets and went in. We took our seats in the front row. First of all, a young girl came in. She had an umbrella in her hand. She walked on a rope. Then some more girls joined her. They showed various feats in gymnastics. They looked like rubber dolls. One of the girls jumped through a fire-ring. Then there were animal shows. An elephant drank water from a bottle. A lion and a goat played with each other. A monkey drove a mini-cycle. The show came to an end at 7 p.m. I liked it very much.
body.plain: A circus came to our town last month. I went to see it with my parents. We bought tickets and went in. We took our seats in the front row. First of all, a young girl came in. She had an umbrella in her hand. She walked on a rope. Then some more girls joined her. They showed various feats in gymnastics. They looked like rubber dolls. One of the girls jumped through a fire-ring. Then there were animal shows. An elephant drank water from a bottle. A lion and a goat played with each other. A monkey drove a mini-cycle. The show came to an end at 7 p.m. I liked it very much.

body.html: 1. <u>Advantages of Early Rising</u>
body.plain: 1. Advantages of Early Rising

body.html: Early rising is a very good habit. It keeps us healthy. There is a popular saying – “Early to bed and early to rise, makes a man healthy, wealthy and wise”. It makes us able to work hard. Early hours are good for our body and mind as well. The air is then cool and fresh. It is very useful for our lungs. It gives them strength. A walk in the early hours keeps us physically fit. A student can study better in the early hours. It is very calm at that time. There is no noise. One can learn better and quickly in the early hours. An early riser can start his work earlier than others. In this way, he can work more. In short, early rising is the best way to rise in one's life.

body.html: 2. <u>A Birthday Party</u>
body.plain: 2. A Birthday Party

body.html: My friend, Anil, gave a party on his birthday. He invited all his friends. The party was held at his house. I reached his house at 6 p.m. There was a great hustle and bustle. A big cake was placed on a table. All of us stood round the table. Anil cut the cake with a knife. All of us chanted three times: "Happy Birthday to You." Then we set to eating. The cake was served to all. It was very tasty. There were many things to eat. We ate to our heart's content. There was singing and dancing also. All of us enjoyed this party. I came back home at 8 p.m.

body.html: 3. <u>A Marriage Party</u>
body.plain: 3. A Marriage Party

body.html: My elder brother's marriage took place last month. The marriage party went to Patna. We went by bus. We went laughing, singing and dancing. We reached the bride's house at 7 p.m. It was nicely decorated. We were given a warm reception. We were served with sweets, tea and cold drinks. We ate to our heart's content. After this, there was the marriage ceremony. The priest recited mantras. The | bride's friends teased my brother much. But our brave hero kept smiling. After the marriage ceremony the elders blessed the new couple. My father refused to take any dowry. All praised him. We came back in the morning.

body.html: 4. <u>A Street Quarrel</u>
body.plain: 4. A Street Quarrel

body.html: Last evening, I was sitting near the window of my room. I saw two children playing in the street. Suddenly they fell out. Other boys of the street gathered there. None tried to separate them. They kept looking on. Soon the mothers of both the children reached there. They started abusing each other. They used dirty words. From hot words they came to blows. They pulled each other's hair. Luckily an elderly woman came there. She separated the fighting ladies. She spoke to them very wisely. The two women realised their mistake. They went back to their homes. Both the children started playing once again.

body.plain: Young students are generally not asked to make up a story. The plot of each story is given, more or less fully, in the form of outlines. But an outline is only a skeleton; you have to clothe this skeleton with flesh and breathe life into it. To do it, the following suggestions should be followed:

body.html: Before you begin to write the story, form a clear idea of its plot in your mind.
body.plain: Before you begin to write the story, form a clear idea of its plot in your mind.

body.html: Follow the outline given; i.e., do not omit any point, and keep to the order in which the points are given in the outline.

body.html: Be careful to connect the points given in the outline naturally; your composition should not look disconnected and jerky. You must use your imagination to fill in the details of the story.

body.html: THE WISE KING
body.plain: THE WISE KING

body.html: Once there arose a dispute between two women. Both of them claimed the same child. One said that she was the child's real mother. The other said, “No, you are a liar. I gave birth to this child”. There was no one who could stand witness for either of them. So it was very difficult to settle the dispute.
body.plain: Once there arose a dispute between two women. Both of them claimed the same child. One said that she was the child's real mother. The other said, “No, you are a liar. I gave birth to this child”. There was no one who could stand witness for either of them. So it was very difficult to settle the dispute.

body.html: Both them agreed to go to the king for justice. The king was very wise. He heard their statements. Each of them claimed to be the child's mother. At last the king said, "There can be only one solution to your problem. The child should be cut in two halves. Each of you can have half the child."
body.plain: Both them agreed to go to the king for justice. The king was very wise. He heard their statements. Each of them claimed to be the child's mother. At last the king said, "There can be only one solution to your problem. The child should be cut in two halves. Each of you can have half the child."

body.html: On hearing this, one of the women kept quiet. But the other one began to weep bitterly. She fell at the king's feet. She requested him not to divide the child. She was willing to give up her claim. She said, "My Lord, give the child to this woman but don't cut it." This affection was a clear proof that she was the child's real mother. The king gave his judgement. The child was given to its real mother. The other woman was sent to jail for her wickedness.

It develops quickness of eye and limb. It calls your thinking power into action. A few sets of tennis in the evening keep me physically and mentally fit.

body.html: For indoors, chess is the queen of games. People say chess is a slow game. But no chess-player ever says this. For an outsider it may look slow to see two men sitting silent and making a move only now and then. But to the two players, it is all the time intensely exciting. There is no game that so absorbs you as chess.

body.html: Anil told me this morning that you had been knocked off your bicycle by a tonga yesterday and badly hurt. I am awfully sorry; but I hope it is not really as bad as Anil made out. If you can write, please let me know how you are. These tongawallas are awfully careless beggars. I had a nasty spill myself a few weeks ago in the same way. Happily no bones were broken. Mind you let me know how you are getting on.

body.html: Dear Yogesh,
body.plain: Dear Yogesh,

body.html: It was with real sorrow when I heard this morning of your great loss. I knew your mother was ill, but I never thought her illness was so serious. The news of your mother's death came to me as a shock. I know you will feel it deeply, for you always loved her so truly. I too feel it as a personal loss to me; she was always very kind to me. Her death must be a terrible grief to your father, too. Please assure him also of my true sympathy.

body.html: Words, I know, are poor comforters. “The heart knoweth its own sorrow,” and in such sorrows we are always alone. But it is not mere words when I say that I feel with you in your sorrow.
